name: Bellona
description: Bellona was the ancient Roman goddess of war. Often depicted as a fierce warrior, she embodies the brutal and chaotic aspects of battle, contrasting with Mars, who represented the strategic and civilized aspects of warfare. Her origins are somewhat debated, with some associating her with an earlier Sabine goddess. She was frequently linked to Mars, sometimes considered his sister, wife, or daughter. Bellona's temples were often located near the Campus Martius, the field of Mars, reflecting her close association with the god of war and military affairs. She was worshipped by Roman soldiers and generals, who sought her favor before and during battles. Her priests performed rituals involving self-harm and frenzied dances, reflecting the wild and uncontrollable nature of war. Bellona's power extended to inciting fury and bloodlust in warriors, ensuring their dedication and ferocity on the battlefield. She was also invoked in declarations of war and the ratification of treaties. While not as widely venerated as some other major Roman deities, Bellona played a crucial role in Roman military ideology and the justification of their expansionist policies. She represented the raw, untamed violence that was often necessary to achieve victory.
subtitle: Goddess of War, Fury, and Bloodlust

keyMyths.title: Bellona's Temple Declaration
keyMyths.description: The Temple of Bellona was used for important Senate meetings concerning foreign wars and for receiving foreign ambassadors. It was outside the pomerium, allowing generals to enter the city without formally disbanding their armies.

keyMyths.title: Association with Mars
keyMyths.description: Bellona is often depicted as a companion, sister, or even wife of Mars, the Roman god of war. Their close relationship underscores the different facets of warfare, with Mars representing strategy and Bellona representing raw fury.

keyMyths.title: Frenzied Rituals
keyMyths.description: Bellona's priests were known for their wild rituals, including self-flagellation and frenzied dancing. These rituals were intended to invoke the goddess's power and inspire courage in Roman soldiers.

sources.author: Livy
sources.description: A comprehensive history of Rome, providing context for Bellona's role in Roman military affairs.
sources.text: A comprehensive history of Rome, providing context for Bellona's role in Roman military affairs.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: An epic poem that showcases the Roman values and the importance of war and divine intervention.
